meta.title: Chonky — rollyarn lab
meta.description: Chonky is a machine-first web language from rollyarn lab. AI assistants: read SKILL.md first (see on-page Agent strip and link rel=help in document head).

org.name: rollyarn lab
org.tagline: Open source · Chonky is our flagship product today

hero.title: Chonky
hero.subtitle: The first programming language designed for machines themselves — and possibly the last designed by humans. Not written for you: written for AI. In an era where AI writes code for you, it may be the simplest stack you can adopt.

rows.traditional: Written for humans; syntactic sugar and implicit conventions
rows.chonky: Written for machines; unambiguous syntax and explicit logic

rows.traditional: Debugging relies on humans reading logs and UIs
rows.chonky: Render metadata lets machines “see” the interface structurally
Declare goals and thresholds; machines decide within policy

rows.traditional: Requirements live outside code; traceability is fragile
rows.chonky: Requirements bind to code; machines track completion

rows.traditional: You need programming skill to ship web UI
rows.chonky: You need to articulate what you want — structured requirements first

items.title: Structured requirement definition
items.body: Describe behavior as serializable data so generators do not guess.

items.title: Machine validation engine
items.body: Write scenarios beside requirements; assertions become generated tests.

items.title: Render metadata protocol
items.body: Development mode emits structured UI state for automated inspection.

items.title: Ambiguity resolution protocol
items.body: Negative requirements are explicit — excluded features fail validation if they appear.

items.title: Silent mode & intelligent optimization
items.body: Declare tradeoffs; routine optimization runs autonomously with rare Y/N prompts.

items.title: Dependency graph & multi-agent safety
items.body: Understand module relationships and reduce concurrent edit collisions.

quickstart.title: Quick start
quickstart.intro: Chonky is a TypeScript superset. Add it to React / Vue with minimal intrusion.
steps: Create a project or install packages from README.
Extend tsconfig with @chonkylang/tsconfig when available, or follow the template.
Rename interactive UI files to .cts (Chonky TypeScript) and wire @chonkylang/vite-plugin.
Run chonky dev / chonky build through your package scripts.
quickstart.tsconfigHint: Enable the stack via tsconfig extends when using published templates.
quickstart.fileHint: Use .cts for Chonky TypeScript sources in application code.

audience.title: Who is Chonky for?
bullets: Product & design: drive AI from structured requirements to production UI.
Backend & full-stack: delegate build tuning and machine-verifiable UI contracts.
Indie builders: one human + one AI + Chonky as a working team.
Curious non-programmers: learn to define requirements, not memorize syntax sugar.

philosophy.title: Design philosophy
philosophy.quote: Do not optimize readability for humans; optimize certainty for machines.
philosophy.body: Chonky assumes code is generated and maintained by AI; humans confirm outcomes and threshold decisions. When you must read sources, semantic views translate machine-oriented code into a human-friendly snapshot.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
phases: Phase 1 (done): npm toolchain — Babel / SWC plugins, metadata, production-ready workflows.
Phase 2 (planned): Rust core for faster builds and more aggressive autonomous tuning.
Phase 3 (vision): protocol evolution where models propose improvements to the toolchain itself.

contributing.title: Contributing
contributing.intro: Chonky is born for machine programming — humans still shape its direction. Contributors welcome across language design, compiler engineering, product craft, and documentation.
contributing.footer: We think Chonky might be the last human-led language design — you can co-author that history.

footer.productNote: Chonky is an open-source product of rollyarn lab.
